reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Grounded in code: models/dinov2-image-features/worker.js loads the REAL transformers.js image-feature-extraction pipeline (Xenova/dinov2-small, WASM, q8) through the shared loadPipeline() and reads the image with RawImage.read(), drives the pipeline's OWN pipe.processor + pipe.model, and reads the raw last_hidden_state — token 0 ([CLS]) is the global descriptor and the patch tokens give the heatmap — a genuine on-device forward pass, never a canned result. The immutable conformance suite tests this seam: calls-real-inference (source must match pipeline(|createModelLoader|extractor|classifier|reranker — pass) and loads-clean (console-clean, no uncaught errors on load — pass). A self-SUPERVISED vision transformer (no labels/text). The worker returns BOTH the L2-normalised 384-d [CLS] global descriptor AND a patch-feature grid (each patch token's cosine to [CLS]) — computed from the real tensors, no second download, no invented API.
rubric.notes: Retrospective backfill: mechanism verified from source + the existing conformance suite; no fresh latency/output numbers were re-measured in this pass (none fabricated).

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a 384-d [CLS] image descriptor (L2-normalised) + a square patch-similarity grid; the page renders an image-to-image cosine similarity matrix across uploaded images and a patch-feature heatmap overlay. Conformance io-shape-semantic is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: gridSideFor() trims to the largest clean square so the heatmap is robust to register/extra tokens.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/dinov2-small), the real pipeline task (image-feature-extraction), backend (WASM — no WebGPU required), quantisation (q8), download size (~24 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). Conformance declares-model-id / declares-task / declares-backend / declares-quantisation all pass against these strings.
rubric.notes: At-a-glance names image-feature-extraction on a self-supervised ViT and the [CLS] + patch-token mechanism — no invented API.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'Xenova/dinov2-small', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:24, requiresWebGPU:false}); a valid current on-device cache auto-initialises with no Load click, and only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source createModelLoader) passes; auto-init-on-cached is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/dinov2-small so the ~24 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/running/done state is announced in a role=status aria-live=polite region; failures surface ON THE PAGE as an .err status ('…failed: …'), not only the console; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path. Runs on the WASM path everywhere, so runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds (no GPU gate). Conformance errors-on-the-page + live-status-region pass; honest-progress-retry-offline is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Scored 4 (not 5): progress/retry/offline are honestly wired but were not re-exercised offline in this retrospective backfill.

rubric.evidence: Real keyboard-operable controls: image upload (file input), sample images, per-image remove buttons (aria-label='Remove …'), and the inspect <select>; results are an image-image cosine matrix + heatmap overlay. No dead controls.
rubric.notes: Scored 4: controls read as real and keyboard-operable from source, but tap-target sizing has an open automated flag (see accessibility).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the [CLS] descriptors, the image-image cosines and the patch heatmap all derive from the real DINOv2 forward pass in the worker. Conformance no-fake-output is manual-evidenced.

Every button exposes an accessible name (conformance buttons-have-names pass) and status uses role=status aria-live. Uploaded thumbnails carry alt=filename, the heatmap overlay carries alt='Selected image with patch-feature heatmap overlay', and the image list is labelled 'Images to compare'. HOWEVER models.json records an automated pre-scan flag for this demo: desktop 16 + mobile 14 sub-24px target(s) — the highest in this batch — sub-24px tap targets an agent still has to verify, and support is 'needs-review' on both classes. Honest weakness, not yet cleared.
rubric.notes: Light+dark use the WCAG-AA shared design system. The sub-24px tap-target flag is the reason this is 4/minor, not 5.

rubric.evidence: The immutable suite carries responsive-desktop and responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) assertions and the shared design system collapses panels to one column on narrow viewports. BUT support.desktop and support.mobile are both 'needs-review' (not yet confirmed 'ok' by an agent matrix pass) and the automated scan flagged desktop 16 + mobile 14 sub-24px target(s) — the highest in this batch. The image-comparison grid + heatmap must stay usable on narrow screens; the elevated sub-24px count (mobile 14) makes an agent tap-target check especially warranted here.
rubric.notes: Scored 4: parity is plausibly fine but UNVERIFIED in this backfill; parity-both-classes remains manual-evidenced/awaiting an agent verdict.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated Web Worker; the page renders only bounded reductions (an image-image cosine matrix + a bounded per-patch heatmap over a small grid) — no main-thread inference and no unbounded per-token loop. Conformance inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced; inference-off-main-thread (source) passes.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — the model forward pass never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work to web worker; break up long tasks so the UI stays responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive (INP): move heavy work off the main thread and avoid long blocking tasks.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — every inference call runs in a dedicated module Web Worker (worker.js) via loadPipeline(); the page only posts messages and renders results, so tokenisation + the model forward pass never block the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread pipeline; conformance inference-off-main-thread (source new Worker|worker.js) pass + inp-stays-low

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long synchronous loops and DOM updates to keep the main thread free
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break heavy synchronous processing / DOM updates so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the heavy work (model forward pass) is already off-thread in the worker; the on-page reductions (similarity matrix / projection / score rendering) are bounded over a small number of items, not unbounded per-token loops on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: bounded on-main-thread rendering; worker owns the model pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout flexible grid no horizontal overflow intrinsic sizing for a control panel and results
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the shared design system (public/styles.css) lays the panels out with flexible grids that collapse to one column on narrow viewports; results scroll inside their own overflow-x container rather than pushing the page wide.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are in the immutable suite

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas-rendered heatmap content accessible name to assistive technology; image alt text
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name so assistive tech can perceive it.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the patch-feature heatmap canvas overlay carries alt/aria text ('Selected image with patch-feature heatmap overlay'); uploaded thumbnails and their remove buttons carry accessible names (aria-label='Remove …', alt=filename).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: index.html alt='Selected image with patch-feature heatmap overlay' + aria-label='Remove ${it.name}'

openQuestions: support.desktop/mobile are 'needs-review' and this demo has the HIGHEST sub-24px tap-target count in the batch (desktop 16 / mobile 14) — a tap-target pass is the priority weakness to clear.
The patch heatmap is drawn to a canvas — is its alt/aria text sufficient, or should the per-patch values also be exposed as a text/table alternative?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass to verify + fix tap targets (this demo has the most sub-24px flags in the batch); consider a text alternative for the canvas heatmap values.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded critique for dinov2-image-features (Xenova/dinov2-small, image-feature-extraction, WASM q8, ~24 MB, Apache-2.0). Real off-thread inference drives the pipeline's own processor+model, returning the genuine 384-d [CLS] descriptor and a real patch-feature heatmap (patch↔CLS cosine) — verified from source + the immutable conformance suite; image-image cosine matrix + heatmap overlay UI with alt/aria text. Genuine weakness flagged HIGHER here: this demo has the most sub-24px tap-target flags in the batch (desktop 16 / mobile 14) and support is 'needs-review' (accessibility/responsive 4/minor, follow-up priority high). No fabricated numbers.
